mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits through lower discount rates and easier financial conditions, lifting S&P 500 prices over the next 20 trading days when the easing move is sustained across at least one monthly observation.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward returns are not higher than baseline after monthly declines in FEDFUNDS, or if the sign of the effect is negative in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above 8.0% signals labor-market stress and deteriorating household income expectations, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day returns more than 60% of the time, or if the average forward return after UNRATE crosses 8.0% is not below unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent slowdown in RSAFS and HOUST indicates weakening final demand and housing-linked spending, which should reduce S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations get revised down.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST does not outperform either series alone in predicting negative 60-day returns, or if positive returns dominate during joint downturns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO combined with stable or falling CPIAUCSL indicates improving real output without renewed inflation pressure, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if episodes with rising INDPRO and non-accelerating CPIAUCSL do not deliver above-average 20-day gains, or if the interaction term is not stronger than each proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates above 2% year-over-year while FEDFUNDS remains near zero, real rates fall and equity duration becomes more valuable, supporting S&P 500 gains over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation pace rises above 2% while FEDFUNDS < 0.5%
failure_condition: Reject if the trigger does not precede positive 5-day returns more often than not, or if high inflation with near-zero rates instead coincides with negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in GDPC1 over the latest quarterly prints signals demand contraction and earnings risk, which should generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 falls on the latest quarterly release versus the prior quarter
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDP declines are followed by positive 60-day returns more often than negative ones, or if the average forward return is not materially below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in PAYEMS over a monthly span signals payroll losses and weaker consumer income, which should pressure S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines for 2 consecutive monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if two-month PAYEMS declines do not precede negative 20-day returns more often than positive returns, or if the signal becomes neutral in rolling walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL creates a tightening real-rate shock that compresses valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint increases in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL fail to predict below-baseline 20-day returns, or if the effect reverses sign during tightening periods.
